design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot transforming mech to purchase units, airlift them across battlefield, issue tactical orders, manage fuel/ammo, capture outposts for revenue generation, and micromanage unit deployment in real-time combat
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time decision-making under resource constraints (fuel, ammunition, unit costs) versus opponent's simultaneous actions; no pause for tactical reflection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a transforming mech as both direct combatant and logistics coordinator, orchestrating ground forces across contested territory
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Combines arcade-style piloting with strategic layer; enforces real-time play with no pauses; mech serves as mandatory command interface bottleneck—all unit management flows through single pilotable unit, creating friction that defines tactical depth. Rejects separation of combat and logistics. Predates and influenced entire RTS genre (Warcraft, StarCraft, Dune II, C&C)

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: AI difficulty scales via armor/damage multipliers on advancing levels; player controls army composition (8 unit types with speed/anti-air/ground-attack/cost tradeoffs) and tactical redeployment strategy

progression.upgrade_system: Unit composition selection and tactical reassignment during individual missions; armor/damage scaling for AI opponents across difficulty levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty levels unlock through advancing single-player campaign; multiplayer available from start
replayability_hooks: Two-player competitive multiplayer with 'fierce rivalry' noted in reviews
Multiple difficulty levels with stat scaling
Strategic variation from 8 unit type combinations and mission order options
Nine outpost control points create variable tactical scenarios

world_building_techniques: Mech design (variable fighter aesthetic referencing Macross)
Battlefield layout with permanent outpost structures
Unit diversity suggests military conflict context

faction_indicators: Player vs. AI/Player vs. Player commander split
Outpost control creates territorial dominance markers
Unit composition choices signal tactical philosophy
danger_signals: Enemy mech positioning near player's main base
environmental_storytelling: Mech damage state reflects engagement history
Fuel depots and supply vehicles indicate logistics network

sources_used: Wikipedia - Herzog Zwei (Gameplay section, Reception section with contemporary and retrospective reviews)
